violations.text: Thermometers are lacking in the walk-in cooler and refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
Refrigerator door gasket of the walk-in entrance near the open bacon unit observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gasket is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the rack in the dairy reach-in have an accumulation of dairy residue and other debris. Rack is to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Produce was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Produce that requires refrigeration was moved to different unit at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

Refrigerator door gasket for the dairy cooler was observed in poor repair. Door gaskets are to be in good repair. In addition, PIC indicated at beginning of inspection that some open-air cases are temporarily shut off while establishment is waiting for parts for repair. No TCS foods are to be stored in these units until equipment is functioning properly. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2024

The doors to the outside, located at the front emergency door and the door near receiving, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023
